steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage notes that despite Bogotá residents having closer parks, their park usage rate is lower. This contradicts a simple proximity explanation, implying another factor must drive the difference.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — implies unmet expectations: Frames the finding as surprising relative to expectations, but the passage is making a logical point about causation, not about what researchers expected. ✗
  • Choice B — focuses on data sources: Brings in the origin of the data, which the passage never raises as an issue. ✗
  • Choice C — attributes the difference to a factor other than proximity: Directly captures the passage's logic — proximity alone doesn't explain the gap, so something else does. ✓
  • Choice D — suggests the data is inaccurate: The passage treats the usage data as valid; it draws a conclusion from it rather than questioning it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
